_description: Coolant loop configurations for chip-level and rack-level cooling

_sizing_notes.specific_heat: c_p affects how much temperature changes with heat input. Water: 4186 J/(kg·K). For faster thermal response and larger control authority, use lower c_p (600-1200). This simulates a less effective coolant or smaller effective heat exchange.
_sizing_notes.flow_rate_range: The ratio m_dot_max/m_dot_min determines control authority. A ratio of 10:1 (e.g., 0.05-0.5) gives good dynamic range. At min flow: T_die should approach T_max_die. At max flow: T_die should be well below target.
_sizing_notes.control_volume: V_m3 determines thermal inertia. Smaller V = faster response but may cause numerical instability. Larger V = more stable but sluggish. Time constant τ = (V * ρ * c_p) / (m_dot * c_p) = V * ρ / m_dot.
_sizing_notes.stability_criterion: For numerical stability with dt=1.0s: ensure τ > 2*dt, i.e., V * ρ / m_dot_max > 2.0s
